Coverage does vary by tooth: molars may have a slightly higher patient cost than front teeth, but both are covered at the same percentage. Your plan’s annual maximum remaining is $1,200.

Great questions! Here’s a clear breakdown based on your Blue Cross Blue Shield PPO plan:

1. **80% Coverage:** The 80% coverage generally applies to all root canal procedures, regardless of tooth location (front teeth or molars). However, your patient cost may be a bit higher for molars due to their complexity, but the coverage percentage remains the same.

2. **$50 Deductible:** The $50 deductible is typically charged once per calendar year, not per procedure. Once you pay this deductible for your first covered dental procedure of the year, you won’t be charged it again for additional procedures within the same year.

3. **Multiple Root Canals:** If you have multiple root canals in the same year, you’ll only pay the deductible once (assuming it hasn’t already been met for another procedure). After that, your insurance will cover 80% of the allowed amount for each root canal, up to your annual maximum.
